reasoning: The function tls_process_server_hello (in ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c) is a client-side function that processes (parses and receives) the ServerHello message sent by the server. It does not generate the ServerHello random field — it simply copies the received bytes from the incoming packet into s->s3.server_random at line 1762: 'if (!PACKET_copy_bytes(pkt, s->s3.server_random, SSL3_RANDOM_SIZE))'. The rule requires that the server, when generating the ServerHello random field, must use a secure random number generator for the first 24 bytes. The server-side generation of the random field happens in tls_construct_server_hello (ssl/statem/statem_srvr.c), not in this function. Since tls_process_server_hello is the client-side receiver and not the server-side generator, this rule does not apply to this function.

evidence.key_code_snippet: if (!PACKET_copy_bytes(pkt, s->s3.server_random, SSL3_RANDOM_SIZE)) { /* line 1762 - copies received bytes, does not generate them */
evidence.expected_behavior: The server must generate the first 24 bytes of the ServerHello random field using a secure random number generator when constructing the ServerHello message.
